title: activity — permissions
description: Permission strings this module publishes. Split by guard: `sanctum` for tenant admins + tenant users reading the feed; `platform_admin` for Stackra ops with cross-tenant read authority. Seeded by ActivityPermissionSeeder with is_system=true.

permissions.name: activity.viewAny
permissions.description: List + show activities in the caller's tenant. RBAC-narrowed by `activity.enforce_scope` middleware — the caller sees only activities on entities they hold viewing permissions for. Every authenticated tenant user gets this by default (baseline read).

permissions.name: activity.audit_any
permissions.description: Enumerate activities across the tenant — call `/api/v1/activities/by-user/{user}` for any actor, or bypass the RBAC-narrowed default on the tenant list. Admin-level compliance surface.

permissions.name: activity.retention.manage
permissions.description: CRUD on retention policies. Gated by `activity_retention_extended` entitlement (Enterprise) for retention_days > 90.

permissions.name: activity.export
permissions.description: Trigger an asynchronous CSV export. Gated by `activity_export` entitlement (Medium+) AND this permission.

permissions.name: platform.activity.viewAny
permissions.description: Cross-tenant list. Every read captured in audit context (SOC 2 CC6.1).

notes: `activity.viewAny` is seeded on EVERY tenant-user role — activity is a baseline feature. The RBAC filter in the middleware narrows the actual result set to permitted subjects; the permission just says 'you can read the feed at all'.

`activity.audit_any` is the compliance-officer permission — it BYPASSES the RBAC-narrowing filter, letting the caller see every activity across the tenant regardless of subject-level permissions. Split from viewAny because bypassing RBAC is a serious authority.
There is no `activity.write` permission — writes happen exclusively via the vendor `LogsActivity` trait on domain models. The permission surface exists only for READ authorities.
